message.content:
1. Agentic micropayments let autonomous software pay tiny amounts instantly for APIs, data, and compute, enabling fine-grained machine-to-machine commerce without human intervention.  
2. They reduce upfront commitment by allowing services to be purchased in small increments, which lowers risk and improves cost efficiency for users and agents alike.  
3. They enable real-time pay-per-use pricing, so providers can monetize resources more precisely and customers only pay for what they actually consume.  
4. They expand access to digital services by making very low-cost transactions economically viable, opening new markets that traditional payment rails cannot support well.  
5. They improve automation and scalability by allowing agents to negotiate, purchase, and settle transactions continuously without manual billing workflows.
